inputs.reasoning: Nested repetitions trigger complex state machine traversal in BitState::TrySearch with multiple Push operations and kInstAlt handling via the arg==0 and arg==1 switch cases.
Large counted repetitions create many instructions, forcing iteration through kInstByteRange cases with multiple character matching operations.
Multiple alternations exercise the kInstAlt switch statement with arg==0 and arg==1 cases, testing the re-push mechanism for handling alternate branches.
Named capture groups trigger kInstCapture case with cap() checks and the arg==0 branch for register capture operations.
Character class with ranges generates ByteRange instructions; tests the p < end condition and character matching logic.
Multiple optional sequences with low-priority alternations exercise the main TrySearch loop (while njob_ > 0) with many Push operations.
Nested capture and repetition forces Push operations; the njob_ >= maxjob_ condition may trigger GrowStack when job stack fills during backtracking.
Long alternation chain tests the switch(arg) statement in kInstAlt case, including the default DFATAL path for unexpected arg values.
Anchored pattern with repetition exercises ByteRange matching at string boundaries; tests p < end condition at end-of-string.
Non-capturing group with alternation and greedy/non-greedy behavior exercises kInstAltMatch case with the ip->greedy(prog_) branch selection.
